name: Sollube Climb and Bermeo Coast Loop
name_original: San Juan de Gaztelugatxe – ciruclar desde Bakio por Bermeo
description: Cycle the difficult Sollube Climb and Bermeo Coast Loop, a 21.9-mile route with 2891 feet of elevation gain through the Urdaibai Biosphere R
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Bakio are characterized by the dramatic Cantabrian coast, offering routes with significant elevation changes and panoramic ocean views. The region features a diverse landscape, including rolling hills, river valleys, and areas with vineyards. Cyclists can explore quiet countryside roads that traverse through forests and open terrain. This varied topography provides a range of touring cycling experiences. Best touring cycling routes around Bakio The most popular touring cycling route is Sollube Climb and Bermeo Coast Loop, a 21.9 miles (35.3 km) trail that takes 2 hours 43 minutes to complete, featuring a challenging climb and scenic coastal views. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Viewpoint Bakio Bay – Bermeo Harbour loop from Bakio, a difficult 14.8 miles (23.8 km) path. This route offers expansive views of Bakio Bay and leads to the Bermeo Harbour. Local touring cyclists also love the View of Bakio – Viewpoint Bakio Bay loop from Bakio, a 33.0 miles (53.2 km) trail leading through coastal areas and offering multiple viewpoints, often completed in about 4 hours 12 minutes. Touring cycling around Bakio is defined by the wild Cantabrian coast, challenging mountain climbs, and picturesque river valleys. The network offers options for various ability levels, from moderate to difficult. The routes in Bakio are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 48 reviews. More than 600 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Bakio's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Bakio?
A: There are 17 touring cycling routes around Bakio documented on komoot. The majority of these, 15 routes, are classified as difficult, with 2 routes being moderate. There are no easy routes specifically for touring cycling in this area.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes around Bakio?
A: Touring cycling around Bakio is characterized by a diverse landscape. You'll encounter the dramatic wild Cantabrian coast with breathtaking ocean views, rolling to hilly terrain, and significant climbs through mountains and forests. Some routes also traverse quiet countryside roads through river valleys and past vineyards, particularly those associated with Txakoli wine.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Bakio?
A: The touring cycling routes in Bakio are highly rated by the komoot community, holding an average score of 4.4 stars from over 48 reviews. Cyclists often praise the challenging climbs, the stunning coastal views, and the varied natural features encountered along the way.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in Bakio?
A: Yes, many of the touring cycling routes around Bakio are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1087462593/sollube-climb-and-bermeo-coast-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sollube Climb and Bermeo Coast Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a challenging ride with scenic coastal views, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31886586" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viewpoint Bakio Bay – Bermeo Harbour loop from Bakio&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What are some challenging touring cycling routes for experienced riders in Bakio?
A: For experienced riders seeking a challenge, Bakio offers several difficult routes with significant elevation gain. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20528096" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Bakio – Viewpoint Bakio Bay loop from Bakio&lt;/a&gt; is a demanding 33-mile (53.2 km) route with nearly 1500 meters of ascent. Another excellent option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32005549" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Armintza – Jata Climb loop from Bakio&lt;/a&gt;, known for its challenging Jata Climb.

Q: What natural features or scenic viewpoints can I expect to see along the routes?
A: Bakio's touring cycling routes are rich in natural beauty. You'll experience the wild Cantabrian coast, offering panoramic views of the Bay of Biscay. Routes often include significant climbs like the Sollube, providing expansive vistas. You can also expect to cycle through lush river valleys, dense forests, and picturesque vineyard areas, particularly those producing Txakoli wine.

Q: What landmarks or attractions can I visit along the touring cycling routes?
A: Several iconic landmarks and attractions are accessible or visible from touring cycling routes around Bakio. A must-see is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/275684" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Saint John of Gaztelugatxe&lt;/a&gt;, a hermitage on a spectacular islet. Other notable sights include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5269209" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views over the Urdaibai marsh&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1516121" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Butrón Castle&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/527262" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Matxitxako Lighthouse&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Is there public transport access to the touring cycling routes in Bakio?
A: While specific public transport details for each trailhead are not provided, Bakio is generally accessible by local bus services. It's advisable to check local transport schedules for routes connecting to Bakio and nearby towns, especially if you plan to start or end your ride away from the main town center.

Q: Where can I park my car to access touring cycling trails in Bakio?
A: Bakio offers various parking options, particularly near the town center and beach areas. Many touring cycling routes start directly from Bakio, making it convenient to park your car and begin your ride. For specific trailheads, look for designated parking areas in nearby villages or at popular viewpoints, though these might be limited.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Bakio?
A: The best time for touring cycling in Bakio is generally during the spring (April to June) and autumn (September to October). During these seasons, the weather is typically milder and more stable, with fewer crowds than in peak summer. Summer can also be pleasant, but be prepared for warmer temperatures and potentially more tourists, especially along the coast.

Q: Are there places to eat or drink along the touring cycling routes?
A: Yes, Bakio and the surrounding towns like Bermeo offer various cafes, restaurants, and bars where you can refuel. The region is also known for its Txakoli wine, and some routes may pass by local wineries where you can experience this aspect of Basque culture. It's always a good idea to carry sufficient water and snacks, especially on longer or more remote sections.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes suitable for families or beginners in Bakio?
A: The touring cycling routes listed for Bakio are predominantly classified as difficult, with a few moderate options. There are currently no routes specifically rated as easy for touring cycling. Beginners or families with young children might find the elevation changes and distances challenging. It's recommended to assess your fitness level and experience carefully before attempting these routes.

Q: Can I bring my dog on touring cycling routes in Bakio?
A: While the guide does not explicitly state whether routes are dog-friendly, general outdoor etiquette applies. If you plan to bring your dog, ensure they are well-behaved, on a leash where required, and that you clean up after them. Be mindful of other cyclists and any local regulations regarding pets in natural areas or on specific trails.
tips: Bakio is a beautiful seaside town located on the Basque coast, famous for its golden sandy beach and impressive cliffs. Among its main attractions are the church of San Pelayo, built in the 17th century, and the Ethnographic Museum, which exhibits a collection of objects that reflect the life and customs of the area.

Charming town with a long fishing history. Its picturesque port, its historic center and its delicious local gastronomy make it a unique place. In addition, the natural beauty of its beaches and the coast are an unforgettable experience. Bermeo is a site that you cannot miss if you are in the area.
From the lighthouse you have a 270° view on the coast, very clear on st juan de Gaztelugatxe, perfect spot for some scenic pictures!

The San Juan de Gaztelugatxe Viewpoint is one of the most impressive places in the Basque Country. From there, you can contemplate the impressive views of the sea and the island of Gaztelugatxe, with its famous hermitage at the top.

The hermitage, dedicated to San Juan Bautista, dates from the 10th century and is located on top of a rocky islet connected to the coast by a stone bridge with 241 steps.

faqs.question: How challenging is the Sollube Climb and Bermeo Coast Loop?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as difficult, primarily due to the demanding Sollube Climb. The climb features an average gradient of 8.7% with sections reaching up to 16.9%, requiring good to very good fitness and appropriate climbing gears for touring cyclists. While coastal sections can vary, the combination makes for a challenging and rewarding experience.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is diverse. The Sollube Climb features challenging gradients on generally good road surfaces, though some parts near the summit can be rougher. The coastal sections around Bermeo, particularly within the Urdaibai Biosphere Reserve, are mostly well-paved, winding through charming villages and offering stunning views of the estuary.

faqs.question: What scenic highlights and landmarks can I expect to see along the way?
faqs.answer: The route offers numerous scenic points. You'll pass by Bakio Beach, experience the vibrant atmosphere of Bermeo Harbor, and enjoy panoramic views from the Matxitxako Lighthouse. A notable highlight is the View of San Juan de Gaztelugatxe, offering iconic vistas of the hermitage.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the route?
faqs.answer: While the tour officially starts near Bakio, Bermeo is a popular and recommended starting point for the Sollube Climb due to lower traffic. You can typically find parking in Bermeo, especially near the harbor or in designated town parking areas, to begin your loop.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or fees required for cycling in the Urdaibai Biosphere Reserve?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for cycling through the Urdaibai Biosphere Reserve. However, as it is a protected natural area, cyclists are expected to respect local regulations, stay on marked paths, and practice leave-no-trace principles to preserve its ecological richness.

faqs.question: Is the Sollube Climb and Bermeo Coast Loop suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Given its 'difficult' classification and the challenging gradients of the Sollube Climb, this specific loop is not recommended for beginners. It requires good to very good fitness and experience with significant elevation gains. Beginners might consider easier coastal routes around Bermeo before attempting this one.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to cycle this route is generally during the spring (April-June) and autumn (September-October) months. During these periods, the weather is typically milder, and the landscapes are lush. Summer can also be pleasant, but be prepared for potentially warmer temperatures and more tourists, especially in coastal towns.

faqs.question: Does the route intersect with other well-known cycling paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of several other official cycling paths. You will find yourself on parts of the Bizkaiko bira, the [CIMA BI05] Sollube * Bermeo, and the Uribe trails, among others, adding to the varied experience of the loop.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the route itself is a public road and coastal path, it's primarily designed for touring bicycles. If you plan to bring a dog, it would need to be able to keep pace with a bicycle for a significant distance or be carried in a suitable bike trailer. Always ensure your dog is on a leash when not actively cycling and respect local regulations regarding pets in towns and protected areas.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or get refreshments along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, especially in the towns and villages along the coast. Bermeo Harbor and other coastal settlements offer numerous cafes, restaurants, and shops where you can refuel and enjoy local Basque cuisine. It's always a good idea to carry some snacks and water, especially for the climb itself, as services might be sparse on the mountain sections.

faqs.question: What is the insider tip for tackling the Sollube Climb?
faqs.answer: For the most enjoyable ascent of Sollube, it's highly recommended to approach it from Bermeo. This particular side of the climb is known for having significantly lower traffic volumes compared to other approaches, allowing for a more peaceful and focused climbing experience.
